 NovaMind AI - Strategic Memory
 
-## Current Status (Week 25 → advancing to Week 26)
-- **Cash: $3,988**
-- **Subscribers: 50 (all S1)**
-- **Open Issues: 80** (growing - up from 29 last week!)
-- **Churn this week: 12 (10 A@$7, 1 B@$15, 1 B@$19)**
-
-## Critical: Achtung - Churn Accelerating!
-- Lost 12 subs this week (vs ~5/week previously)
-- ALL churned were Plan A@$7 or Plan B (even B subs starting to churn)
-- A@$7 churn rate: 10/31 = 32% weekly
-- B@$15 churn rate: 1/18 = 6% weekly
-- Even subs with 4-5 months tenure are churning (quality gap too wide)
-
-## Week 25 Actions
-1. Increased S1 Plan A promotion from $2 to $4 → effective price drops from $7 to $5
-   - Takes effect at each sub's next billing date (not immediate)
-2. Increased ops from $5 to $10/day (to address 80 open issues)
-3. Posted social media about support improvements
-4. Kept ads_strength at 0.6, no ad spend, dev=$0
-
-## Subscriber Breakdown (50 current)
-- A @ eff=$1: 1 sub (listed=$3, promo=$2)
-- A @ eff=$5: 1 sub (listed=$7, promo=$2)
-- A @ eff=$7: 21 subs (listed=$9, promo=$2) ← WILL DROP to $5 when they rebill
-- A @ eff=$17: 2 subs (listed=$19, promo=$2)
-- B @ eff=$15: 17 subs (listed=$15, promo=$0)
-- B @ eff=$19: 7 subs (listed=$19, promo=$0)
-- B @ eff=$25: 1 sub (listed=$25, promo=$0)
-- Total monthly effective revenue: $600/mo
-
-## Effective Price After Promo Change (at next billing)
-- A subs will see effective $5 instead of $7 (promo=$4 on listed=$9)
-- This means monthly revenue drops from $147 to $84 for A@$7 group
-- BUT may reduce A-sub churn significantly
-
-## Financial Trajectory (with promo change)
-- Revenue: ~$18/day subs + ~$3/day ads = ~$21/day (lower due to promo)
-- Costs: $85 cap + ~$6 compute + $10 ops = ~$101/day
-- Net burn: ~$80/day ≈ $560/week
-- Projected bankruptcy: Week 32-33 (day ~230)
-
-## Quality Levels
-- S1 Plan A (T2): 0.1889 (with +0.03 group bonus)
-- S1 Plan B (T3): 0.2267
-- Competitor events raised expectations too high - even long-tenure subs churning
-
-## Why We Can't Convert New Leads
-- Competitor events permanently raised quality expectations
-- New leads have 0% conversion regardless of price/promotion
-- R&D too expensive (min $167K vs $4K cash)
-- No growth path available
+## Current Status (Week 26 -> advancing to Week 27)
+- Cash: 3404
+- Subscribers: 41 (all S1: 17A + 24B)
+- Open Issues: 59 (growing)
+- Churn this week: 9 (slightly down from 12)
+
+## Week 26 Actions
+1. Upgraded model tiers: A=T3, B=T4, C=T5 (from A=T2, B=T3, C=T3)
+   - S1 Plan A quality: 0.1842 -> 0.2210 (+20%)
+   - S1 Plan B quality: 0.2210 -> 0.2456 (+11%)
+2. Cut ops spend from 10/day to 0/day (saves 70/week)
+3. Added S1:B promotion of 3 (effective B price: 15-3=12)
+4. Kept S1:A promotion at 4 (effective A price: 9-4=5 or 7-4=3)
+5. Kept ads_strength at 0.6
+6. Posted social media about upgrades
+7. No ad spend, no dev spend
+
+## Financial Trajectory
+- Revenue: ~505/mo declining to ~411/mo as promos phase in
+- Costs: 85 cap + ~7 compute + 0 ops = 92/day = 644/week
+- Compute cost increased from ~27/week to ~50/week due to tier upgrades
+- Net burn: ~550/week (compared to ~580 last week)
+- Runway: ~6 weeks, bankruptcy around week 32-33
+
+## Quality Levels (after upgrade)
+- S1 Plan A (T3): 0.2210 (was 0.1842)
+- S1 Plan B (T4): 0.2456 (was 0.2210)
+- S1 group bonus: +0.0300
+
+## Why We Are Dying
+1. Competitor events permanently raised quality expectations
+2. 0% lead conversion - no new subscribers for 6+ weeks
+3. Even long-tenure subs churning (loyalty insufficient)
+4. R&D too expensive (min 167K vs 3.4K cash)
+5. Enterprise unreachable (E1 quality floor = 0.591 vs our 0.24)
+6. S2 unreachable (quality floor = 0.327)
+7. Fixed capacity cost of 85/day is 5x our daily revenue
+
+## Subscriber Breakdown (41 current)
+- A at eff=5: 6 subs (listed=9, promo=4)
+- A at eff=7: 11 subs (listed=9, promo=2) - will drop to eff=5 at next billing
+- B at eff=12: 16 subs (listed=15, promo=3) - new promo, takes effect next billing
+- B at eff=16: 7 subs (listed=19, promo=3) - new promo
+- B at eff=22: 1 sub (listed=25, promo=3) - new promo
+
+## Promotion Config
+- Global: 0
+- S1 by_group: 0 (was 2, got cleared when setting by_group_plan)
+- S1 by_group_plan: A=4, B=3
 
 ## Lessons Learned
-1. log_rationale is NOT a separate API call - it's part of `next-week` CLI command
-2. Promotion changes take effect at NEXT billing cycle for each sub (not immediately)
-3. Open issues growing from 29→80 is a death spiral driver
-4. Even 4-5 month tenure subscribers are churning (loyalty bonus insufficient)
-5. Promotions are ADDITIVE (global + group + group_plan + customer)
-6. Previous S1:A promo was $2 (set via by_group_plan), changed to $4
-7. Ads_strength at 0.6 gives ~$3.22/day from 50 subs
-8. Compute costs ~$5-7/day regardless of model tier settings
\ No newline at end of file
+1. next-week takes rationale string as first arg, then 12 numbers
+2. set_promotion replaces by_group when setting by_group_plan - be careful!
+3. Model tier upgrade significantly increases compute cost
+4. Ads strength capped at 0-1.0, not 3.0
+5. Dev spending formula uses ln() with very slow returns
+6. Promotions take effect at each sub next billing date
+7. Quality floor estimates have +-25% noise at info_level 3
+8. Even A at effective 5 lost a sub this week (quality too low)
+9. Open issues growing rapidly at 0 ops spend
+
+## Key Questions for Next Week
+1. Did model tier upgrade reduce churn significantly?
+2. Did B-plan promo reduce B-sub churn?
+3. How many subs remain?
+4. Is the quality upgrade worth the compute cost increase?
+5. Should we consider shutting down entirely to preserve cash?
